Procedure plan:

- Use read_file to get fstab.
- Write backup: write_file to /etc/fstab.bak with same content.
- Prepare awk script: 
```
awk '
    BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"}
    {
        if($0 ~ /^#/ || $0 ~ /^$/ || $2 != "/boot") {print; next}
        old=$4; 
        split(old,opts,","); found=0; 
        for(i in opts) if(opts[i]=="nodev") {found=1; break}
        if(!found) {
            if(old=="" ) {$4="nodev"} else {$4=old ",nodev"}
        }
        print
    }
' /etc/fstab > /tmp/fstab
```

But cannot use multiline. However, run_cmd can run a single shell command that includes subshells or multiline? The instructions: Do NOT chain with && ; or multiline scripts. But can pass command string that includes newlines? Usually it's okay but might be considered multiline. Safer to use a single-line command using single quotes and newline escape `\n`. We can craft one-liner with ' ' and escape \n. But be careful.

Maybe easier: use sed:

`sed -i '/\s\/boot\s/ {
s/\(nodev\)\s*//g
s/$/ nodev/
}' /etc/fstab`

This removes nodev duplicates then add one. But still duplicates? That might add duplicate if nodev already present removes then add a single. But ensures not duplicate.

Better choose:

`sed -i '/\s\/boot\s/{
s/[^[:space:]]*//g
}'` can't.

Maybe use more precise: `sed -i '/\s\/boot\s/ s/\bnodev\b//g; /^\s*#.*\/boot/! {s/$/ nodev/}'` hmm.

Given complexity, maybe easiest: Use awk script but format as single line with -v.

We can craft command:

`awk 'BEGIN{FS=OFS="\t"} $2!="/boot"||/^\s*#/ {print; next} {split($4,a,","); found=0; for(i in a) if(a[i]=="nodev") found=1; if(!found) {if($4=="") $4="nodev"; else $4=$4",nodev"} print}' /etc/fstab > /tmp/fstab`

This is one line. Good.

We can then use write_file to replace /etc/fstab. Use write_file with content from /tmp/fstab? But write_file expects content string, not reading file. We can read it using read_file: read_file /tmp/fstab, then write_file /etc/fstab.

So commands:

- run_cmd cp /etc/fstab /etc/fstab.bak
- run_cmd awk '... > /tmp/fstab'
- read_file /tmp/fstab
- write_file /etc/fstab with content.
- run_cmd mount -o remount /boot

Also maybe remove /tmp/fstab after.

Edge: backup must happen before modifications. Good.
